title: Could Mattias Ekholm return for the Edmonton Oilers? + Jason Demers
summary: The Edmonton Oilers are officially halfway to the ultimate goal. With two playoff rounds down and two still to go, it’s the perfect time to pause and reflect on what’s been an unforgettable postseason journey so far. On today’s episode of Oilersnation Everyday, we’ll take a step back and evaluate everything that’s brought the Oilers to this point. How has their game evolved since the puck dropped on Round 1? We’ll also look ahead to Round 3, discussing potential lineup combinations, the injury status of Mattias Ekholm, and the looming goaltending decision between Calvin Pickard and Stuart Skinner. We’ll deliver our One Big Take, ask the Sherwood Ford Giant Question, and go around the rest of the league with our NHL Playoff Report as the Final Four begins to take shape. To wrap things up, we’ll serve up some weekend picks with help from our friends at Bet365. The Oilers are heating up—and if the second half of the playoffs is anything like the first, we’re in for a wild ride. title: The Edmonton Oilers are headed back to the Western Conference Finals
summary: The Edmonton Oilers are heading back to the Western Conference Final—again. It only took five games to dispatch the Vegas Golden Knights, as the Oilers shut out them out 1-0 in an intense, defensive Game 5 showdown. It was playoff hockey at its tightest, with both teams playing a conservative, high-pressure game from puck drop to the final buzzer. But it was the Oilers who found the breakthrough. After 60 minutes of scoreless hockey, the game headed to overtime where Kasperi Kapanen played the hero. The veteran winger crashed the crease and banged home a loose puck at the side of the net, giving the Oilers the win and officially punching their ticket to the Western Conference Final for the third time in the last four seasons. It was another masterclass from Stuart Skinner, who recorded his second straight shutout—stopping all 24 shots he faced. He now sits at 127 consecutive minutes of shutout hockey and is peaking at the right time. On today’s episode of Oilersnation Everyday, we’ll break down everything from Game 5. From our full scoring summary to the biggest moments of the night, we’ll take you through how the Oilers got it done. We’ll give you our One Big Take, tackle the Sherwood Ford Giant Question, and go around the rest of the NHL as the second round winds down and we wait to see who Edmonton’s next opponent will be. The job isn’t finished, but the Oilers have made a major statement—and the city of Edmonton is buzzing. Let’s talk about it. title: The Edmonton Oilers are 1 win away from the WCF + Bruce Curlock
summary: The Edmonton Oilers are staring down a massive opportunity tonight in Las Vegas. After dropping the Game 3 of this second-round series, the Oilers delivered one of their best performances of the season, and now have a 3-1 series lead heading into Game 5 at T-Mobile Arena. They’re one win away from advancing to the Western Conference Final—and if their performance in Game 4 is any indication, they’re trending in the right direction. Stuart Skinner silenced critics and delivered his best performance of the postseason in Game 4, stopping all 23 shots he faced to secure his second career playoff shutout. The Oilers’ depth continues to shine, with Adam Henrique scoring twice and Evander Kane chipping in with another gritty goal. It was a statement night from Edmonton, and now they have a chance to finish off the Golden Knights in their own building. But Vegas won’t go quietly—expect a desperate, physical response from a team fighting to keep its season alive. On today’s episode of Oilersnation Everyday, we’ll preview every angle of Game 5. We’ll have our full lineup report, break down key game notes, deliver our Sherwood Ford Giant Question, give you our One Big Take, and check in with the Bet365 Gameday Betting Challenge. Can the Oilers replicate the effort and composure they showed in Game 4? And what kind of storm will they need to weather from Vegas early on? Later in the show, we’ll be joined by Oilersnation writer Bruce Curlock to dig deeper into what’s gone right for the Oilers in this series, how Stuart Skinner has responded under pressure, and whether or not he believes Edmonton will close out the series tonight—or if this thing is heading back to Rogers Place for a Game 6. title: Edmonton Oilers Game 3 Breakdown & Game 4 Preview + Louie DeBrusk
summary: The Edmonton Oilers had a chance to take a 3-0 series lead on Saturday night… until disaster struck. Reilly Smith scored the game-winning goal for the Golden Knights with just 0.4 seconds left on the clock, stunning Oil Country and stealing Game 3 in dramatic fashion. It was a crushing end to what started as a great night for the Oilers, who jumped out to an early 2-0 lead in the first period before Vegas clawed their way back into the game. Connor McDavid tied it late to give Edmonton life, but the Oilers now face a pivotal Game 4 tonight with the series at 2-1. On today’s edition of Oilersnation Everyday, we’ll recap everything that happened in Game 3—what went wrong, who stepped up, and how things fell apart in the dying seconds. We’ll dig into the controversial final sequence, the Oilers’ defensive lapses, and the decision to go back to Stuart Skinner for Game 4. Confidence in the crease is once again a major storyline, and we’ll give our take on whether the Oilers are making the right call between the pipes. We’ll also look ahead to tonight’s massive Game 4 matchup, as the Oilers still have a chance to take a 3-1 stranglehold on the series before heading back to Vegas. What do they need to change? What’s working? What’s not? We’ll cover it all with our full game day lineup report, game notes, our Sherwood Ford Giant Question, one big take, and the Bet365 betting challenge. Plus, Sportsnet analyst Louie DeBrusk joins the show to break down the heartbreak from Game 3, weigh in on the goaltending decision, and give his thoughts on what the Oilers need to do tonight to bounce back and grab control of this second-round series. title: The Edmonton Oilers take a commanding 2-0 lead over Vegas + Dan Tencer
summary: Game 2 between the Edmonton Oilers and Vegas Golden Knights delivered exactly what everyone expected—drama, intensity, and another heart-pounding finish. On today’s episode of Oilersnation Everyday, we’re breaking down a wild 5-4 overtime win for the Oilers, who now return to Edmonton with a commanding 2-0 lead in the series. Leon Draisaitl played the hero in overtime, burying the game winner after a brilliant setup by Connor McDavid, who absolutely undressed Jack Eichel to create the chance. It capped off another chaotic playoff performance where the Oilers’ depth once again came through in a big way. The fourth line stole the show as Vasily Podkolzin and Viktor Arvidsson each registered two-point nights, proving that this team isn’t relying solely on its superstars to get it done. Calvin Pickard had arguably his most important game as an Oiler, stopping 28 of 32 shots and coming up with several massive saves in the third period and overtime. His poise under pressure continues to be one of the biggest stories of the playoffs for Edmonton. We’ll go through our full scoring summary, deliver our One Big Take, ask the Sherwood Ford GIANT Question, and take a spin around the league with our NHL Playoff Report. Joining us on the show will be Shawn DePaz, producer of What Chaos!, to break down that OT winner and the electric McDavid play that made it all happen. We’ll also hear from Dan Tencer, scout for the Saskatoon Blades, who will give his insight into the Oilers' dominance so far, what’s gone wrong for Vegas, and whether the Golden Knights should be hitting the panic button heading into Game 3. The vibes are high in Oil Country—tune in for a full breakdown of a massive Game 2 win. title: Can the Edmonton Oilers Take a 2-0 Lead in Vegas? + Jason Gregor
summary: Tonight, the Edmonton Oilers will look to take a 2-0 stranglehold in their second-round series against the Vegas Golden Knights, and we’ve got everything you need to know ahead of Game 2 right here on Oilersnation Everyday. The Oilers clawed back from an early 2-0 hole in Game 1 and powered their way to a 4-2 win on the road thanks to contributions up and down the lineup. We’ll dive into what made that win possible and what they’ll need to repeat heading into tonight’s crucial matchup. We’ll kick off the show with our full lineup report, including another look at Connor McDavid and Leon Draisaitl staying together on the top line — a move that continues to create chaos for opponents. We’ll also talk about the return of elite depth scoring from guys like Corey Perry, Connor Brown, and Ryan Nugent-Hopkins, and how that could be the key to pushing the Oilers deeper into the postseason. Plus, we’ll examine John Klingberg’s reliability and steady play on the blue line, which has helped settle things down defensively. In goal, Calvin Pickard once again gets the nod after another composed, confident performance in Game 1. We’ll talk about his quiet consistency and whether he’s locking down the crease as the team’s playoff starter moving forward. As always, we’ll run through our Game Notes, hit you with our Sherwood Ford GIANT Question, and give you One Big Take ahead of puck drop. We’ll also go around the league in our Boston Pizza Playoff Report, as the NHL’s second round continues to heat up. And of course, we’ll wrap with the latest edition of the Bet365 Game Day Betting Challenge, as Jay Downton looks to stay hot with his playoff picks. title: Who shot Greg LeMond?
summary: Welcome to the first episode of Rabbit Holes, presented by Escape Collective.
